# `spatial_awareness.ron` — field reference

<!-- GENERATED by `cargo run -p dc_data_docs` (dronecom #2815). Do not edit: field docs live as /// comments on the Rust structs. -->

Data: [`config/spatial_awareness.ron`](../config/spatial_awareness.ron)

## `SpatialAwarenessConfig`

Configuration for the spatial awareness grid.

Loaded from `assets/config/spatial_awareness.ron`.

### `quadtree_depth` : u32

Terrain quadtree depth level that determines grid cell size.

### `update_interval_secs` : f32

How often the spatial map refreshes (seconds).

### `coverage_half_life_secs` : f32

Exponential decay half-life for coverage values (seconds).

### `threat_weight_surface` : f32

Threat weight per `NtdsClass` variant.

### `threat_weight_air` : f32

Threat weight for a fixed-wing air (`NtdsClass::Air`) contact (unitless).

### `threat_weight_helicopter` : f32

Threat weight for a helicopter (`NtdsClass::Helicopter`) contact (unitless).

### `threat_weight_subsurface` : f32

Threat weight for a submarine / subsurface (`NtdsClass::Subsurface`) contact (unitless).

### `threat_weight_land` : f32

Threat weight for a land (`NtdsClass::Land`) contact (unitless).

### `threat_weight_command_ship` : f32

Threat weight for a command-ship (`NtdsClass::CommandShip`) contact (unitless).

### `threat_weight_missile` : f32

Threat weight for a missile (`NtdsClass::Missile`) contact (unitless).

### `threat_weight_torpedo` : f32

Threat weight for a torpedo (`NtdsClass::Torpedo`) contact (unitless).

### `threat_weight_unknown` : f32

Threat weight for an unclassified (`NtdsClass::Unknown`) contact
(DC-1050) — no class evidence yet, so it could be anything from a
scout to a missile. Tuned between the surface baseline and the
high-threat weapon classes.

### `scouting_coverage_threshold` : f32

Coverage below this (`scouting_coverage_threshold`) triggers "needs scouting".

### `scouting_land_fraction_max` : f32

Skip cells with `land_fraction` above `scouting_land_fraction_max` for scouting.

### `prior_threat_max` : f32

Maximum prior threat seeded in cells within enemy team sectors.
Higher values make the AI more eager to scout toward enemy territory.

### `overlay_render_distance` : f32

Maximum distance (metres) from the camera at which overlay cells are drawn.
Cells beyond this distance are culled to avoid horizon line artifacts.
